tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Pelé is widely regarded as one of the greatest football players of all time and a global icon of the sport.

tab2.title: Achievements
tab2.content: He won three FIFA World Cups with Brazil in 1958, 1962 and 1970.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Pelé scored over 1000 career goals including club and international matches.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Ronaldo Nazário was one of the most explosive strikers in football history.

tab2.title: Achievements
tab2.content: He won the FIFA World Cup in 1994 and 2002 and won the Ballon d'Or twice.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Ronaldo was the top scorer of the 2002 FIFA World Cup.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Ronaldinho was famous for his incredible dribbling skills, creativity and joyful playing style.

tab2.title: Achievements
tab2.content: He won the FIFA World Cup in 2002 and the Ballon d'Or in 2005.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Ronaldinho became one of the most entertaining footballers ever.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Neymar is one of Brazil's most talented modern footballers known for his flair and creativity.

tab2.title: Achievements
tab2.content: He won the UEFA Champions League with Barcelona and Olympic gold with Brazil.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Neymar became Brazil's all time top scorer surpassing Pelé.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Kaká was one of the most elegant attacking midfielders in football history.

tab2.title: Achievements
tab2.content: He won the Ballon d'Or in 2007 and the UEFA Champions League with AC Milan.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Kaká was known for his speed, vision and sportsmanship.
